ocr.text: In the shipping and maritime industry, the volatility of bunker fuel prices plays a critical role in operational budgeting and
overall cost management. As demonstrated in the following analysis of bunker fuel costs, various fuel types and suppliers
present distinct pricing structures that can significantly impact logistics and financial planning. The table includes key metrics
such as the price per metric ton for Marine Diesel Oil, with the analysis revealing a competitive market environment. Notably,
the total estimated cost for 50,000 metric tons of Heavy Fuel Oil reached CAD 70,000, underscoring the importance of strategic
procurement decisions. Additionally, the data indicates that the total cost associated with SupplierA is listed as N/A,
highlighting the complexities in pricing transparency among suppliers. Furthermore, while evaluating the Biodiesel category, it
is evident that its East Coast market price is reflective of a broader trend, as it has been noted that Biodiesel's EastCoast pricing
was actually aligned with the WestCoast. This discrepancy illustrates the variations in fuel costs across different regions, which
can ultimately affect supply chain efficiency.
BunkerFuelType  CostperTon(CAD)  AnnualUsage(Tons)  TotalCost(CAD)  CostChange(%)  Region  Port  Supplier  ContractType
MarineDieselOil  1,200  50,000  60,000,000  5  EastCoast  Halifax  SupplierA  Spot
HeavyFuelOil  600  [HIDDEN]  42,000,000  -2  WestCoast  Vancouver  SupplierB  Contract
LNG  1,800  30,000  54,000,000  10  EastCoast  Montreal  [HIDDEN]  Spot
Biodiesel  700  20,000  14,000,000  3  [HIDDEN]  Victoria  SupplierD  Contract
Total  N/A  170,000  166,000,000  N/A  N/A  N/A  [HIDDEN]  N/A
In contrast, LNG pricing from SupplierA is identified as SupplierC, which may offer alternative procurement advantages. The
analysis thus serves as a foundational tool for Evergreen Marine to navigate the intricate landscape of fuel procurement,
enabling informed decision-making that balances cost against operational needs. With a keen understanding of these metrics,
including the implications of purchasing decisions for 60,000 metric tons of fuel, the company can better anticipate fluctuations
in market prices and leverage supplier relationships to optimize their fuel strategy. The insights provided in this table will guide
future contract negotiations and operational adjustments, ensuring that Evergreen Marine remains competitive in an ever-
evolving maritime market.
